text.title: AI + Spatial Computing Accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ai-spatial-computing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: All user-facing AI inference for interactive XR surfaces MUST run on-device (Core ML / Sentis / ONNX / MediaPipe). Cloud AI only for non-real-time tasks.
rule.rationale: Cloud round-trips cannot meet the 20 ms comfort threshold.

rule.statement: Every interactive surface MUST document three-tier input fallback: gaze → voice → controller/gesture. No single-modality dependency.
rule.rationale: Gaze fails for glasses + nystagmus; voice fails in noise; gestures fail for limb differences.

rule.statement: Measured p95 inference latency on the target device MUST be <20 ms for every real-time interaction.
rule.rationale: Above 20 ms users experience cognitive friction in XR.

rule.statement: Eye / face / hand / room-mesh / voice capture MUST present a GDPR/CCPA consent screen before first use and document a retention period.
rule.rationale: Biometric data has strict legal status (BIPA, GDPR Art. 9).

rule.statement: Release MUST be blocked until at least one user with the relevant disability has tested on the actual target device.
rule.rationale: XAUR audit alone is structured opinion; physical accommodation cannot be verified remotely.

rule.statement: Every XAUR clause cited by the agent MUST be cross-checked verbatim against the W3C source.
rule.rationale: XR a11y is thin in LLM training data; hallucinated clauses are common.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
